methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected data, including ANOVA, NMDS, ANOSIM, α-diversity, β-diversity, Mantel test, Levin's niche breadth, Spearman's correlation, and PLS path analysis.

steps.description: One-way ANOVA was employed to compare the differences in sediment physical and chemical properties using SPSS (version 23.0, IBM Crop., Armonk, NY, USA).
steps.searchable_summary: perform one-way ANOVA using SPSS to compare sediment properties

steps.description: Non-metric multi-dimensional scaling (NMDS) analysis based on Bray-Curtis dissimilarity and analysis of similarities (ANOSIM) were conducted to evaluate the complexity of the community composition and compare the differences between sampling regions.
steps.searchable_summary: perform NMDS and ANOSIM using R vegan package

steps.description: The α-diversity was calculated using the 'picante' package.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate α-diversity using R picante package

steps.description: β-diversity was quantified as the average distance of group members to the group centroid in the vegan package based on the Bray-Curtis distances.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate β-diversity using R vegan package

steps.description: Mantel test was executed to evaluate the relationships between the βNTI of the bacterial community and sediment environmental properties.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Mantel test to evaluate relationships between βNTI and sediment properties

steps.description: Levin's niche breadth of the species was calculated using the 'spa' package to demonstrate the community sensitivity to the sediment environment.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Levin's niche breadth using R spa package

steps.description: Assessed the relationships among the sediment properties, network features and community diversity employing Spearman's correlation and Mantel test.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Spearman's correlation and Mantel test on sediment properties and community diversity

steps.description: Performed the partial least squares (PLS) path analysis using the 'plspm' package in R.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PLS path analysis using R plspm package

methods.description: Procedures for analyzing physical and chemical properties of sediment samples, including freeze-drying, grinding, sieving, and various chemical analyses.

steps.description: All sediment samples were freeze-dried to constant weights, and roots and stones were removed.
steps.searchable_summary: freeze-dry sediment samples and remove roots and stones

steps.description: Samples were softly grinded in a mortar and then were passed through a 2 mm sieve.
steps.searchable_summary: grind and sieve sediment samples

steps.description: Sediment pH was measured in 2.5:1 mixtures of deionized water and soil using a pH meter.
steps.searchable_summary: measure sediment pH using pH meter

steps.description: Total carbon (TC), total nitrogen (TN), and total sulfur (TS) were measured using an elemental analyzer.
steps.searchable_summary: measure TC, TN, TS using elemental analyzer

steps.description: Dissolved organic carbon (DOC) was extracted using the soil-to-distilled water in a ratio of 1:5 (w/v) and finally determined with a TOC analyzer.
steps.searchable_summary: extract and measure DOC using TOC analyzer

steps.description: Labile Organic Carbon (LOC) was measured based on potassium permanganate oxidation method.
steps.searchable_summary: measure LOC using potassium permanganate oxidation

steps.description: Total sediment phosphorus (TP) content was determined using perchloric acid digestion and a UV-2550 spectrophotometer.
steps.searchable_summary: determine TP using perchloric acid digestion and spectrophotometer

steps.description: The molybdenum blue colorimetric method was employed to determine the concentration of available phosphorus (AP).
steps.searchable_summary: determine AP using molybdenum blue colorimetric method

methods.description: Procedures for extracting DNA from sediment samples and processing sequences for microbial community analysis.

steps.description: Sediment DNA was extracted from 0.5 g wet sediment using a Power Soil DNA Isolation Kit.
steps.searchable_summary: extract DNA from sediment using Power Soil DNA Isolation Kit

steps.description: The extracted DNA was then preserved in −80°C until the next step.
steps.searchable_summary: preserve extracted DNA at −80°C

steps.description: Specific primer pairs combined with the adapter sequence and barcode sequence were used to amplify the distinct regions (V3-V4) of the bacterial 16S rRNA genes.
steps.searchable_summary: amplify V3-V4 regions of 16S rRNA genes using specific primers

steps.description: All PCR reactions were performed with 15 µL of Phusion® High Fidelity PCR Master Mix, 0.2 µM of forward and reverse primers, and approximately 10 ng of template DNA.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCR with Phusion® High Fidelity PCR Master Mix
inputs: Phusion® High Fidelity PCR Master Mix

steps.description: Sequencing was employed on an Illumina NovaSeq platform and 250 bp paired-end reads were generated.
steps.searchable_summary: sequence DNA on Illumina NovaSeq platform

steps.description: The 16S rRNA sequencing data were analyzed using QIIME 2. Paired-end reads were merged using FLASH and were denoised using DADA2 in QIIME 2.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze 16S rRNA data using QIIME 2, merge reads with FLASH, denoise with DADA2

steps.description: Sequences were classified using the SILVA database (v.138).
steps.searchable_summary: classify sequences using SILVA database

steps.description: The amplicon sequence variants (ASVs) were defined using a 97% similarity cutoff.
steps.searchable_summary: define ASVs with 97% similarity cutoff
